intro: Uragoga neuwiedii, also known as the "Wild Ginseng" or "Brazilian Ginseng", is a perennial plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to reduce stress, improve cognitive function, and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga neuwiedii include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenes, such as annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to exhibit potent anticancer and anti-parasitic activities.
